briefdescription.para: Documentation for the core graph implementation.

sect1.title: Introduction
sect1.para: The graph implementation in the core module provides the fundamental data structure for representing physics events as graphs. Each event can be converted into a graph where particles become nodes and their relationships become edges.

sect1.title: Structure
sect1.para: A graph in AnalysisG consists of:

listitem.para: Nodes: Represent particles or physics objects
Edges: Represent relationships or connections between particles
Features: Tensors attached to nodes, edges, or the graph itself

sect1.title: Feature Types
sect1.para: The framework supports three levels of features:

listitem.para: Graph-level features: Properties of the entire event (e.g., missing ET)
Node-level features: Properties of individual particles (e.g., pT, eta, phi)
Edge-level features: Properties of particle pairs (e.g., deltaR, invariant mass)

sect1.para:
Each feature level is further divided into:
Data features (D-prefix): Input features for the model
Truth features (T-prefix): Target labels for training
